laws.title: Public Law 4-32 | Warrants, Subpoenas, and Summons Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Historical warrants, subpoenas, and summons enactment; no current operative text remains.
laws.chronology: Superseded in the source record; no later operative codification target was resolved in the reviewed materials.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete supersession; no surviving transfer or redesignation requiring operative text.
laws.rationale: The source record is superseded and does not support current positive-law text. Historical note treatment is sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 4-33 | National Archives Act
laws.final_statutory_text: General and permanent enactment preserved as a statutory note at 28 U.S.C. section 503; no express Code amendment is identified.
laws.chronology: Current in the source record; no later repeal identified, but the manifest title and Title 44 anchor are not the authoritative placement.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer issue; the source is a note-worthy general enactment, not a Title 44 codification.
laws.rationale: The authoritative source is the Bar Association Reform Act, not the manifest's National Archives Act label. The correct anchor is 28 U.S.C. section 503, and a statutory note is sufficient.

laws.title: Public Law 4-34 | An Act to Prevent the Proliferation of Magistrates and Ensure Basic Judicial Competence
laws.final_statutory_text: Historical judiciary statute; no current operative text remains.
laws.chronology: Fully repealed in the source record.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal; no transfer or redesignation survives.
laws.rationale: The enactment is repealed and should remain historical only.

laws.title: Public Law 4-35 | Recommissioning of the United States Ship New Jersey Act of 2022
laws.final_statutory_text: Source unavailable; no operative text was recovered for positive-law placement.
laws.chronology: Reported as repealed in the canonical record; the attachment itself was unrecoverable.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No determinable transfer or redesignation from the supplied material.
laws.rationale: The source attachment was unavailable, so the law cannot be reconstructed into operative code text. Historical treatment only is appropriate.

laws.title: Public Law 4-36 | Defense Heraldic Services Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Heraldic services provisions already appear in 10 U.S.C. sections 206 and 7594; no new operative text is required.
laws.chronology: Current enactment already incorporated into the Code text.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal, transfer, or redesignation issue; the operative text is already split across the existing heraldic-services sections.
laws.rationale: The law is already absorbed into operative title 10 text, so the review should treat it as incorporated rather than requiring a new section.

laws.rationale: The law is fully repealed and should remain a historical note only.

laws.title: Public Law 4-38 | White House Protection Act

laws.chronology: Fully repealed in the source record.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Complete repeal; no surviving transfer or redesignation.
laws.rationale: The manifest's Title 3 placement is too coarse; the operative subject anchors most naturally in Secret Service powers, but the enactment is now historical only.
